channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Curated breaking geopolitical headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Surge as US-Iran Deadlock Keeps Gulf Supply Offline
item.description: Brent crude extended gains to near $120/barrel as the US-Iran standoff continues to block crucial Middle East oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, with no resolution in sight.

item.title: 🏛️ Powell to Remain on Fed Board, Citing Unprecedented Trump Administration Attacks
item.description: Fed Chair Jerome Powell announced he will stay on as a Federal Reserve governor after his chairmanship ends next month, defending the central bank independence amid what he called unprecedented legal battering by the Trump administration.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Trump Reviews Potential US Troop Reduction in Germany
item.description: President Trump said his administration is studying a reduction of US forces in Germany, citing Berlin failure to send naval assets to help open the Strait of Hormuz during the Iran crisis.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC+ Poised to Raise Oil Output Despite UAE Exit and Iran War
item.description: OPEC+ is expected to agree to a small production increase at its Sunday meeting even as the Iran conflict blocks Gulf exports and the UAE prepares to exit the cartel on May 1.

item.title: ☢️ UN Nuclear Test Ban Chief Warns US and Russia Against Resuming Tests
item.description: The head of the Comprehensive Nuclear Test Ban Treaty Organization warned that if the US or Russia conducts a nuclear test, other nations will follow, potentially starting an unstoppable spiral of proliferation.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippine House committee advances impeachment case against Vice President Sara Duterte
item.description: A congressional justice committee ruled there was "probable cause" to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte over allegations of unexplained wealth, misuse of state funds, and threats to assassinate the president. The case now moves to the full House.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada denies entry to Iranian soccer federation chief for FIFA Congress
item.description: Iranian Football Federation president Mehdi Taj was denied entry to Canada ahead of the FIFA Congress in Vancouver. Canada cited Taj's alleged ties to the IRGC, a designated terrorist entity, as the reason for the visa revocation.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish and Spanish Foreign Ministers Call for Unified Stance Against Israel Gaza Flotilla Interception
item.description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan and his Spanish counterpart Jose Manuel Albares stressed the need for a unified international stance against Israel's illegal interception of the Gaza flotilla.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Three Police Officers Killed in Attack in Southeast Iran
item.description: An attack on a police patrol in Iran's restive Sistan-Baluchistan province killed three officers, amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli Military Chief Vows Strikes Beyond Litani River in Lebanon
item.description: Israel's military chief visited southern Lebanon and vowed to strike Hezbollah targets north of the Litani River, escalating beyond the agreed ceasefire boundaries.

item.title: ⚓ US Navy to Extend Service Life of USS Wasp Amphibious Assault Ship by 5 Years
item.description: The US Navy completed a study on extending the service life of Wasp-class amphibious assault ships and plans to extend the USS Wasp by five years amid heightened global operations.

item.title: 🚢 Hormuz Shipping Traffic Drops to Trickle as US-Iran Deadlock Deepens
item.description: Only six ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz in 24 hours as the US and Iran remain deadlocked over reopening the vital waterway, highlighting the severe impact of the blockade on global energy trade.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese Hackers Spied on Cuban Embassy as US Prepared Blockade
item.description: A hacking campaign compromised emails of 68 officials including the Cuban ambassador, raising concerns about Chinese intelligence operations amid heightened US-Iran tensions.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali Turmoil Threatens Russian Push for Influence in Africa
item.description: Reversals suffered by Mali Moscow-backed military government have dented Russia image as security guarantor in Africa and threaten its strategic and economic interests on the continent.

item.title: ⚖️ Supreme Court Weakens Voting Rights Act, Aids GOP Redistricting
item.description: The Supreme Court has weakened the landmark Civil Rights-era law that increased minority representation in Congress, with significant implications for the upcoming elections.

item.title: 🏦 Powell Fed Era Bookended by Trump Draws to Close
item.description: Jerome Powell prepares to end his term as Fed chair, his legacy debated after navigating the Trump feud, COVID-19 response, and aggressive monetary policy shifts.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 Trump threatens to reduce US troops in Germany amid Iran war feud
item.description: President Trump said he is considering reducing the US military presence in Germany after Chancellor Merz criticized the lack of US strategy in the Iran war and said America was being humiliated by Iranian leadership.

item.title: 🚢⏱️ USS Gerald R. Ford carrier ending record 300-day deployment
item.description: The world's largest aircraft carrier is heading home after a record-setting deployment that included the Iran war and the capture of Venezuelan leader Maduro, marking the longest carrier deployment since Vietnam.

item.title: 🇧🇷🏛️ Brazil Senate rejects Lula Supreme Court nominee for first time in 132 years
item.description: Brazil's Senate rejected President Lula's Supreme Court nominee Jorge Messias in a 42-34 vote, the first rejection since 1894, dealing a political blow to the incumbent ahead of October elections.

item.title: 🇨🇳✈️ China Southern Airlines orders Airbus jets worth $21 billion as Boeing deal remains rumor
item.description: The state-owned airline joins peers in placing large orders with the European manufacturer, while any potential Boeing deal hinges on broader US-China trade dynamics amid tensions over Iran and technology.

item.title: ☢️🇺🇦 US pledges financial support for G7 initiative to repair Chornobyl confinement structure
item.description: The United States commits to continued leadership on nuclear safety and non-proliferation, supporting the G7 effort to maintain the containment shelter over the damaged reactor.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE Abandons OPEC After Six Decades
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has officially left OPEC in a dramatic break from the Saudi-dominated oil cartel. The move reshapes Gulf power dynamics and global energy politics as the UAE seeks independence from Riyadh's influence.

item.title: 🇱🇾⛵ Libya Migrant Boat Disaster: 17 Dead, 9 Missing
item.description: At least 17 migrants died and nine remain missing after their boat broke down and drifted for eight days in the Mediterranean Sea. Libya's Red Crescent reported the vessel had been adrift without proper supplies.

item.title: 🇨🇦🇪🇺 Canada Selected as NATO Defense Bank Headquarters
item.description: Canada has been chosen as the headquarters for a new NATO-led financial institution designed to reduce borrowing costs for alliance members. The initiative aims to strengthen defense financing across member nations.

item.title: 🇬🇧💎 NYC Mayor Urges King Charles to Return Koh-i-Noor Diamond
item.description: During King Charles's state visit, NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani publicly encouraged the British monarch to return the controversial Koh-i-Noor Diamond. The gem remains a symbol of colonial-era disputes between Britain and former colonies.
